Document:
# Testing & Debugging
Tips and techniques for testing your changes in OpenEdge 14.
## Starting the Dev Environment
# Kill any existing server killall Content.Server
# Build first dotnet build -c Release
# Start server (dev mode) bash runserver.sh
# Start client (separate terminal) bash runclient.sh
[bold]Order matters:[/bold] Always kill the server before building. File locks during build will cause errors.
## Common Test Scenarios
### Testing Character Stats 1. Create a character, spend points 2. Open character window (C key) 3. Verify stat values and modifier display
### Testing Spells 1. Learn the relevant skill 2. Equip item with OE14SpellStorage (if applicable) 3. Verify spell appears in action bar 4. Cast spell and verify mana deduction 5. Verify effect on target
### Testing Crafting 1. Gather required materials 2. Approach workbench 3. Open crafting UI 4. Verify recipe is visible and craftable
## Debugging Tips
[bold]Parser errors in guidebook:[/bold] Check XML for forbidden syntax — angle brackets in text, triple backticks, curly braces outside CodeBlock, markdown bold (**text**).
[bold]Network not syncing:[/bold] Verify the field has the AutoNetworkedField attribute.
[bold]Spell not appearing:[/bold] Verify skill is linked to spell, and spell entity exists.
[bold]Server won't start (port 1212):[/bold]
lsof -ti:1212 | xargs kill -9
## Log Locations
Server logs output to console. For guidebook parser errors, look for:
[Guidebook] Encountered error while generating markup controls
## Content Under Construction
Full testing guide with automated test examples coming soon.
